name: Soupe a L'onion
recipeIngredient: 4 slices French bread, cut 1/2 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. In a covered 4-quart saucepan, cook the thinly sliced onions slowly with the butter and 1 tablespoon oil for about 15 minutes. Keep covered, stirring occasionally.
2. Uncover the pan and increase heat to moderate. Add sugar and sauté onions until golden brown, about 30 minutes.
3. Sprinkle onions with flour and stir over heat for 2 or 3 minutes.
4. Blend in hot broth and vermouth, then season to taste. Simmer partly covered for 1 hour.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Place bread slices in the oven for 15 minutes. Remove and baste each slice with half a teaspoon of oil and rub with a cut garlic clove. Return to the oven for about 15 more minutes.
6. Before serving, add cognac to the soup and divide into 4 ovenproof bowls. Sprinkle half a cup of cheese in the soup. Float slices of bread on top of the soup and sprinkle with the remaining cheese. Bake in the oven at 325°F (165°C) for 15 to 20 minutes until hot. Set under broiler for 2 or so minutes until golden brown.
